narrative.context: You feel stressed and exhausted, but you also have an upcoming test. You’re torn between studying more or sleeping.
narrative.prompt: How do you use your emotions to make a better decision?

options.label: Use stress + fatigue to prioritize recovery and quality
options.description: Recognize fatigue will reduce learning. Choose sleep and a short, high-yield revision plan for tomorrow.

ei.rationale: Uses emotional/physiological signals to protect cognition and plan effectively—emotion facilitates thinking.

options.label: Study a little more, then sleep
options.description: Do one focused 20-minute review, then stop.

ei.rationale: Balances goals, though the plan may still be influenced by stress-driven urgency.

options.label: Stay up late to reduce anxiety
options.description: Study until very late because you feel worried.

ei.rationale: Emotion drives overwork; cognition and performance can worsen.

options.label: Avoid both by scrolling
options.description: Scroll endlessly because the decision feels stressful.

ei.rationale: Avoidance prevents emotion-informed planning; reduces outcomes.
